steps.title: Use Text 2's response
steps.content: Text 2 says there is no contemporary evidence that the first audience of Arrival of the Train panicked. It argues that the story persists because people strongly believe film's arrival was culturally transformative.

steps.title: Separate myth from evidence
steps.content: A invents a belief that historians are more sophisticated than today's audiences. B says historians regard the transformative view as naive, though they repeat the myth. D wrongly grounds the story in actual audience alarm. The remaining option captures Text 2's view that the story is driven more by perceived significance than by facts. The answer is C.
